spec_match.rationale: Perfect comparative layout showcasing exactly why hardcoded strategies fail on either the clipped edge or the wasteful edge, whereas env() scales perfectly.

interactive.rationale: Excellent interactive slider updating side-by-side diagrams with custom text reports and warning badges, coupled with a reactive target-row database matrix.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly highlights the engineering trade-offs of printing constraints on the modern web.

edge_cases.rationale: The support note now states the exact limit of the probe: env() fallback syntax parses on screen, while safe-printable-inset resolution is print-context and printer dependent.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the mandatory ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Environment Variables safe-printable-inset spec reference.

design_system.rationale: Clean grids, color-coded status badges (ok, clip, warn) matching public/styles.css principles.

openQuestions.title: Re-review repaired live probe in chrome-devtools-mcp
openQuestions.detail: The live probe or copyable feature-detection snippet was repaired on 2026-06-04. Re-run this route in chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise the visible controls, and verify the repaired probe output plus console/network state before marking the critique fully current.

summary: The printer-compare concept now clears the major reference compliance finding while preserving the interactive comparison of fixed and adaptive print margins. 2026-06-04 maintenance replaced inaccurate live-probe logic with feature-specific checks; chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable in this session, so browser click-through re-review is still required.
